mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management and tuning for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf llm-d-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

llm-d version

```

> Download the latest CLI version.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.
> Verify the installation by checking the version.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Update kubeconfig with cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to verify their status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply the autoscaler configuration.
> Apply the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes llm-d and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Remove the autoscaler.
> Remove the monitoring setup.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d > llm-d-backup.yaml

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/llm-d-v0.5.1.yaml

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current resources.
> Cordon and drain the node before upgrading.
> Apply the new version of llm-d.
> Verify that the new pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Review the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust resource requests/limits in the deployment.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The request to the Kubernetes API server is timing out.

**Fix:**
Check the network connectivity and ensure the API server is reachable.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.5.0, no specific migration steps are required.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in a Running state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
